Aggregated bias · 1-glance synthesis
Today's verdict NEUTRAL-CAUTIOUS
Reconciliation · why the tape contradicts itself
⚠ Signals disagree — Crash Index **1/10** (calm price tape) vs VIX Spike Tier **2/4** at **60%** with skew and backwardation active
Spot price is sleepy while the options curve is paying for a jump. Trust the VIX Spike read for hedging cost and timing, trust the Crash Index for directional exposure; options lead spot at these readings.
⚠ Signals disagree — Regime State **18** STABLE vs Macro Regime BROADENING at only **30%** confidence
Stability score says no regime shift, but the broadening call is low conviction. Trust the stability read for now and treat BROADENING as a hypothesis, not a position.
⚠ Signals disagree — Contrarian score **50** ACCUMULATE (partial) vs Divergence composite ELEVATED, **20** bearish pairs vs **5** bullish out of **43**
A partial accumulate signal sits against a tape where most cross-asset pairs are decoupling bearishly. Trust the divergence picture for sizing discipline; the accumulate signal is half-cooked.
Today's tension
Price action is calm while options are quietly pricing a vol expansion.
Bull
The Crash Index sits at **1/10** and regime state scores **18** as STABLE, with US small caps leading at **+3.9%** over 5 days. That breadth thrust through the **^RUT** says risk appetite is broadening, not breaking.
Bear
The VIX Spike Detector is at **Tier 2/4** with a **60%** probability, skew and backwardation both active, and divergence breadth sits ELEVATED with **58.1%** of pairs decoupling. Options are paying up for protection the tape has not earned yet.
The tell: Watch the SKEW vs VIX pair. Correlation drifted from **-0.26** to **-0.81**, and a re-coupling with VIX itself lifting flips the calm read fast.
AI read · Citrini · today's macro tape

The tape is calm on the surface and noisy underneath. Crash Index reads 1/10, regime state scores 18 STABLE, and ^RUT leads the US at +3.9% over 5 days while ^GSPC lags at +0.6%. Small caps leading large caps in a quiet tape is the broadening signature the macro regime call is trying to name, even at only 30% confidence.

You lean long risk, but you pay for the vol you would normally skip. VIX Spike Tier 2/4 with a 60% probability, skew active, backwardation active, says the options market is bidding protection while spot drifts. That is the cheapest moment to hold hedges, because the broadening tape gives you the upside and the curve gives you a discount on the downside. Position size up in US small caps and US-listed risk, fund the hedge with the calm in spot.

The risk is a divergence resolution that goes the wrong way. 58.1% of 43 cross-asset pairs are diverging, with 20 bearish against 5 bullish, and that is an ELEVATED reading. The tell is the SKEW vs VIX pair, where correlation drifted from -0.26 to -0.81: if SKEW keeps screaming while VIX itself lifts, the calm score flips and you cut gross.

5-regime heuristic classifier · cross-asset
Macro Regime — where are we structurally
AI read · macro regime

Macro regime reads BROADENING at 30% confidence, which is a low-conviction call for a real signal. Broadening means leadership widens past the largest names into small caps and laggard sectors, and ^RUT at +3.9% over 5 days against ^GSPC at +0.6% is the receipt. You position with a tilt toward US small caps and equal-weight exposure, but you size it like a hypothesis at 30% conviction, not a regime call you stake the book on. If small caps keep leading for another week and credit stays tight, confidence climbs and you add.

SPY above its 200-DMA with contained vol. This is the zone where long convexity compounds: leverage and call buying carry the lowest variance drag. Size up your highest-conviction directional calls here.
Gate from Leverage for the Long Run (Gayed/Bilello 2016): the durable edge is the 200-DMA × vol regime, the 3× compounding is marketing. Attention gauge for call sizing, not a trade trigger.

Dow Theory generalized · cross-asset divergences
Cross-asset divergence radar — Cross-asset divergence watch
AI read · divergences in plain English

Cross-asset divergence is ELEVATED, with 58.1% of 43 pairs decoupling and a net bearish bias of 15 (20 bearish vs 5 bullish). Divergence means assets that usually move together are no longer doing so, and correlation drift is the speed of that change. The loudest jaws are SKEW vs VIX going from -0.26 to -0.81, HYG vs IEF from +0.30 to +0.84, LQD vs IEF from +0.09 to +0.82, and SPY vs TLT 60-day from +0.13 to +0.74. Credit and rates are re-coupling hard while options-internal pairs are tearing apart, and that mix says the bond complex is moving as one block while the volatility surface is repricing on its own clock.

Cross-asset confluence · live status
Confluences — most proven alpha of the framework 0/3 ACTIVE
AI read · what the confluences mean now

Zero confluences are firing today, with 0 active out of 3 tracked. VIX backwardation is live, but it has not paired with HYG credit stress, with small-cap drawdown, or with a JPY safe-haven bid. Backwardation alone is a yellow light, not a red one. You keep risk on, you keep the hedges you already bought, and you wait for a second leg (credit widening or a yen bid) before you act on the vol signal as a directional trade.

✅
T1 × HYG (VIX backwardation + Credit stress)
Volatility backwardation meeting credit stress — the highest-conviction pre-correction setup in the framework. Fires only when options fear and the bond market confirm each other.
STAND-BY
🟢 VIX3M/VIX backwardation1.160
🟢 HYG/IEF stressZ=+1.13
✅
T1 × B2 (VIX backwardation + Small-cap drawdown)
Volatility backwardation with small-caps breaking down — breadth thinning while vol rises. A late-rally warning when the index masks weakness underneath.
STAND-BY
🟢 VIX3M/VIX backwardation1.160
🟢 IWM/SPY breakdownZ=+2.37
✅
T1 × JPY (VIX backwardation + Yen safe-haven flow)
Volatility backwardation with the yen bid — safe-haven flow confirming the vol signal. Classic risk-off rotation when carry trades unwind.
STAND-BY
🟢 VIX3M/VIX backwardation1.160

🌍
Asia · Europe · US · Crypto 24/7 — lead-lag detection
Cross-Region Surveillance
Major indices from the 4 regions/asset classes. Asia trades ahead time-wise vs US — a Tokyo overnight crash often leaks into the SPX open. Crypto = 24h leading indicator of global risk appetite.
AI read · the cross-region tape

The 24/7 tape says US is leading and Korea is the global pain point. ^RUT at +3.9% over 5 days runs the world, while ^KS11 sits at -10.1% as the laggard everywhere. Europe is mixed with ^SSMI at +1.4% against ^GDAXI at -2.9%, and crypto is flat with BTC-USD at +0.3% and ETH-USD at -1.6%. The read for the US session: small caps are the marginal buyer globally, Korea idiosyncratic weakness is not bleeding into Bitcoin or the broader risk tape, and you treat US strength as confirmed until ETH or HYG starts to crack.

01
Forward-looking indicators · Bonferroni-validated
Crash Index — 10 lead-signal radar 1/10 · WATCH — 1-2 signals, monitoring
AI read · volatility & crash, reconciled

Crash Index at 1/10 and VIX Spike at Tier 2/4 with 60% probability is a coherent state, not a bug. Spot is calm because realized vol has not arrived, and the options market is paying up for it anyway through active skew and active backwardation. That combination, low realized plus a bid term structure, is the classic setup where hedges are mispriced cheap relative to the regime the curve is forecasting. You trust the Crash Index for your directional book and the VIX Spike for your hedge book; they answer different questions.

CRASH INDEX
1/10
WATCH — 1-2 signals, monitoring
10 forward-looking indicators · Bonferroni-validated
DXY z>1.5 structural
lead 30-90d ❌ z=+1.13
DXY+SKEW combo structural
lead 45-346d ❌
SKEW>140 (Tail Risk) structural
lead 1-94d ✅ 142.6
SKEW>155 structural
current regime ❌ 142.6
VIX3M/VIX<1.0 stress
lead 5-20d ❌ 1.16
STLFSI4 d20 stress
lead 5-30d ❌ -0.22
HYG/IEF z<-1.5 stress
lead 5-20d ❌ z=+1.13
T10Y2Y resteepen stress
recession warning ❌ +0.00
SPY -2% in 5d trigger
lead 5-15d ❌ +0.57%
VIX +30% in 3d trigger
lead 1-10d ❌ -11.02%
Score 0-2 CALM · 3-4 WATCH · 5-6 WARNING · 7-8 ALARM · 9-10 EXTREME (rare crash imminent). Lead times: structural 30-90d · stress 5-30d · triggers 1-10d. Four Telegram alerts available depending on escalation.
How to read
Each row = an independent signal Bonferroni-validated over 15 years of SPY. The structural (DXY, SKEW, MOVE) signal a regime change over 30-90 days. The stress (HYG/IEF, VIX3M/VIX, STLFSI4) ring at 5-30 days. The triggers (SPY -2% in 5d, VIX +30% in 3d) are short-term breakouts. For a tactical decision: 3+ signals simultaneously aligned = real probability of stress, never on isolated signal (framework rule).
02
Regime score · 6 bearish signals (Bonferroni + walk-forward)
Regime State 18 · STABLE — no regime shift
18
STABLE — no regime shift
Aligned 3+d 8/20
Yield steepening 0/20
MOVE Z-score 3/20
STLFSI momentum 0/20
Credit stress 0/20
🆕 DXY surge 7/20
How to read
Score 0-100 aggregated from 6 sub-signals (each 0-20) that predict SPY underperformance. Bonferroni-validated over 15 years, walk-forward robust. Above 50 → active-bearish regime (trim winners, tight stops). DXY surge added in May 2026 (current regime 2023-2026, strong dollar = toxic for global risk-on).
03
Contrarian score · 3 buy signals (Bonferroni + walk-forward)
Contrarian Buy 50 · ACCUMULATE — partial signal
50
ACCUMULATE — partial signal
VIX capitulation 0/50
VIX3M/VIX = 1.16 (backwardation = capitulation)
Inflation regime 50/50
corr SPY-TLT 60d = 0.54
🆕 SMH BTFD (regime current) 0/50
SMH/SPY Z=+2.06 (<-1.5 → +11% 60d test 2023-26)
Contrarian Score = signals that predict SPY OUTPERFORMANCE (capitulation buy). VIX backwardation → +7.29pp SPY at 120d · Corr SPY-TLT>0 → +3.88pp at 120d · SMH z<-1.5 → +11pp at 60d (test 2023-2026). Reading: score > 70 = aggressive fear-buy setup.
How to read
Bonferroni MCPT backtest 2026-04-30 over 15 years SPY · 9/15 tests VALID. Regime Score vs Contrarian Score are independent — they can coexist high (regime transition) or low (structural calm). Always read the combination, not each one alone.

How to read
Four validated signals from the framework (libs kerberos_*), surfaced here so the cockpit reflects what the doctrine flags today: Max Optimism Stack (4 sentiment components + 3d buffer + haven filter) · RSI Breadth zones (capitulation vs euphoria) · xMarketTopMas (high-beta vs duration risk appetite) · Composite Divergence (cross-asset pairs diverging). Full stats on the Backtest page.

3 confluence components (Validated, regime 2023-2026)
1. SKEWACTIVE
142.6
Trigger: > 140 (tail-hedge demand)
2. BackwardationACTIVE
VIX3M/VIX = 1.160
Trigger: > 1.05 (vol curve inverted)
3. Adaptive (proxy)INACTIVE
VIX 17.7 · ratio 1.16

Probability VIX +50% within 60d
60%
vs baseline 37% · backtested cycle 2023-2026
Action — sizing tied to tier
Buy hedges B-tier (1-2% book) — VIX calls 30-60 DTE strike 25-28 OR SPY puts 60-90 DTE Δ0.20-0.30
How to read
4 tiers (BROAD / MEDIUM / HIGH / EXTREME) based on the confluence of 3 Validated components: SKEW > 140 (tail-hedge demand), VIX3M/VIX > 1.05 (vol curve inverted), VIX < 16 + ratio > 1.15 (adaptive proxy). Each tier indicates a historical probability of VIX +50% at 60d (cycle backtest 2023-2026, vs baseline 37%). Strict sizing: tier 2 = 1-2% book hedges, tier 3 = 3-5% long-vol, tier 4 = 5-10% MAX (rare confluence).
05b
Under-the-hood vol · Observation (Imran Lakha)
Vol Microstructure — VIX beta & term structure
VIX spot
17.68
VIX beta 20d
-9.19
0th pct
VIX beta 60d
-3.20
0th pct
VIX9D / VIX
0.976
contango
VIX3M / VIX
1.160
contango
VVIX / VIX
5.31
vol-of-vol
VIX beta 20d collapsed (-9.19, 0th pct) — historically VIX becomes a buy. BUT only if VIX catches a bid on the next spot down-move. If VIX stays asleep, the setup is dead (Lakha's caveat).
How to read
VIX beta = how much VIX moves per unit SPX move (rolling regression). Collapsed beta (low pct) = VIX asleep → historically a buy, but worthless unless VIX re-engages on the downside. VIX9D/VIX > 1 & VIX3M/VIX < 1 = backwardation (stress). Observation only — no sized signal.

06
Aggregated bias · 53-ratio composite
Regime synthesis — RISK-ON / OFF / STRESS / TOPPING
MIXED — competing signals No clean composite per Kerberos. Wait for alignment.
RISK-ON aligned (3)
• Small caps leading
• Copper > Gold
• Industrials > Utilities
RISK-OFF aligned (1)
• ETH dumping vs BTC
STRESS aligned (0)
none
TOPPING aligned (1)
• SMH stretched extreme
How to read
Aggregation of all panel signals across the 4 cross-asset buckets. RISK-ON = risk appetite (cyclical, equity, EM). RISK-OFF = flight to defensives (TLT, gold, JPY). STRESS = liquidity tension (HYG, MOVE, STLFSI). TOPPING = end-of-cycle signals (concentration, breadth narrowing, defensives leading). Doctrine: a single signal = noise. 3+ aligned in one bucket = real confirmed regime.

07
VOL TERM STRUCTURE · 5 ratios
Volatility — VIX-family ratios
VIX3M / VIX
Stretch +0.66
Volatility term structure
1.1601 +5.28%
◆ RULE READ · AI joins tonight
This ratio compares 3-month fear (VIX3M) to immediate fear (VIX). Above 1, traders anticipate more stress in 3 months than today — that's the normal situation. Below 1, it's the opposite: immediate panic more expensive than long-term insurance. Current reading: 1.16 — normal calm regime. The curve is in typical contango, neither panic nor excessive complacency. Action: no directional signal, watch for a break below 1.00 (panic) or above 1.30 (top warning).
VVIX / VIX
Stretch +0.01
Vol of vol
5.307 +2.51%
◆ RULE READ · AI joins tonight
VVIX = volatility of the VIX itself. When it rises sharply, it means institutionals are actively buying insurance against a crash (deep puts, VIX calls). It's an internal stress signal from the options market. Current reading: Z=+0.01 — no particular stress in options. Average insurance level, neither complacency nor panic. Action: no directional reading, follow the other signals.
VIX9D / VIX
Stretch +0.50
Ultra short-term panic
0.9762 -8.14%
◆ RULE READ · AI joins tonight
VIX9D = 9-day fear, VIX = 30-day fear. When the 9-day exceeds the 30-day, it means an immediate shock is anticipated (often around an event: Fed, election, earnings). Current reading: 0.98 — no imminent short-term stress. The market is not pricing a shock at 9 days. Action: no immediate defensive reading.
SKEW
Stretch -0.10
Black swan insurance
142.6 -0.27%
◆ RULE READ · AI joins tonight
The SKEW index measures how much institutionals pay to protect against an extreme SPX crash (puts far out of the money, what's called 'tail-hedge'). Above 140, demand for protection is strong. Current reading: 143 — moderate demand zone for crash insurance. Big players are starting to hedge, no panic yet. Action: watch for a move above 150 = full alarm, otherwise stay neutral.
SKEW / VIX
Stretch +0.63
Tail-risk premium relative to vol
8.07 +9.66%
◆ RULE READ · AI joins tonight
SKEW/VIX compares the cost of extreme crash insurance to the cost of standard volatility insurance. When this ratio rises, it means tail fear is higher than daily fear — an interesting divergence. Current reading: Z=+0.63 — standard level, no gap. Tail insurance cost aligned with normal vol. Action: no signal.
VIX / Realized Vol 30d
Stretch -0.78
Vol regime persistence — chop vs crash detector
1.32 -7.82%
◆ RULE READ · AI joins tonight
Z=-0.78, last value 1.3152811233074924. No specific Kerberos reading defined for this pair.
08
CREDIT SPREADS · YIELD CURVE
Credit & rates — risk-on/off proxies
HYG / IEF
Stretch +1.13
High yield credit stress
0.8488 +0.17%
◆ RULE READ · AI joins tonight
HYG = high-yield bonds (junk bonds), IEF = US sovereign bonds 7-10 years. The ratio measures the health of risky credit: when HYG underperforms IEF, smart money is fleeing credit risk before it hits equities. Current reading: Z=+1.13 — balanced credit, no junk stress. The bond market is flagging nothing unusual. Action: no defensive reading from credit.
LQD / IEF
Stretch +1.58
Investment grade liquidity
1.1575 +0.11%
◆ RULE READ · AI joins tonight
LQD = investment-grade corporate bonds, IEF = 7-10y treasuries. When even quality bonds underperform treasuries, it means liquidity is tightening everywhere, not just on junk. Current reading: Z=+1.58 — quality credit stable. Investment-grade spreads healthy, no macro liquidity stress. Action: no defensive signal from quality credit.
T10Y2Y
Yield curve (10y - 2y)
⚠ Failed to fetch data
T10Y3M
Fed-preferred curve (10y - 3m)
⚠ Failed to fetch data
TIP / TLT
Stretch +0.06
Inflation expectations
1.2780 +0.25%
◆ RULE READ · AI joins tonight
TIPS (inflation-indexed bonds) divided by TLT (long nominal bonds). When TIPS outperforms TLT, the bond market is pricing more inflation to come — risk for equity multiples. Current reading: Z=+0.06 — stable inflation expectations. No inflation panic priced. Action: no signal on multiples.
MOVE
Stretch -0.40
Bond volatility (BofA)
69.4 -0.13%
◆ RULE READ · AI joins tonight
MOVE = implied volatility of Treasury bonds (VIX equivalent for bonds). When the bond market becomes volatile, it's often a precursor to equity stress (bond vol leads equity vol). Current reading: 69 — normal bond vol. No Treasury market stress. Action: no defensive signal from bonds.
Corr SPY / TLT 60d
Stretch +1.70
Equity-bond regime
0.54 -2.01%
◆ RULE READ · AI joins tonight
60-day rolling correlation between SPY (equities) and TLT (long bonds). Deflation regime = negative correlation (bonds = haven, play their hedging role). Inflation regime = positive correlation (both fall together — risk parity under stress). Current reading: +0.54 — inflation regime active. Bonds and equities fall together, the traditional 60/40 no longer works as a hedge. Action: don't count on TLT to hedge SPY, look for other protections (gold, VIX calls, cash).
Δ T10Y2Y 60d
Yield curve steepening rate

09
SECTOR ROTATION · LEADERSHIP
Sectorials & breadth — who's leading
XLY / XLP
Stretch -0.38
Consumer confidence
1.3587 -0.38%
◆ RULE READ · AI joins tonight
XLY = consumer discretionary (cyclical, discretionary purchases), XLP = consumer staples (defensive, necessary purchases). When XLP beats XLY, the consumer is going into cautious mode — often a bearish divergence if SPX keeps rising. Current reading: Z=-0.38 — balanced discretionary/staples. No defensive rotation. Action: no sector signal.
IWM / SPY
Stretch +2.37
Risk appetite (small vs large)
0.3949 +0.33%
◆ RULE READ · AI joins tonight
IWM = Russell 2000 (small caps), SPY = S&P 500 (large caps). When small caps underperform strongly, it means breadth is dying — the rally is only carried by mega-caps. Classic end-of-cycle signal. Current reading: Z=+2.37 — small caps leading the market. Risk-on broadening, signal of a healthy bull market. Action: stay offensive, possible small cap exposure.
SMH / SPY
Stretch +2.06
Tech leadership
0.8358 +1.18%
◆ RULE READ · AI joins tonight
SMH = semis ETF (Nvidia, AMD, etc.), SPY = S&P 500. When semis dominate to the extreme, it's often an exhaustion signal (tech can't outperform indefinitely). When they break down, it's the end of the tech leadership cycle. Current reading: Z=+2.06 — semis dominating at the extreme. Unsustainable tech leadership level, rising mean-reversion pressure. Action: trim NVDA/AMD/SMH, beware of recent entrants.
SPY / RSP
Stretch +0.25
Cap-weighted vs equal-weighted
3.5046 -0.36%
◆ RULE READ · AI joins tonight
SPY = S&P 500 cap-weighted (mega-caps weigh enormously), RSP = S&P 500 equal-weighted (each stock 0.2%). When SPY outperforms RSP, mega-caps are hiding the weakness of the rest — dangerous concentration. Current reading: Z=+0.25 — broad market participation. No dangerous concentration. Action: no signal.
XLI / XLU
Stretch +1.35
Offensive vs defensive
3.9564 -0.50%
◆ RULE READ · AI joins tonight
XLI = industrials (cyclical pro-growth), XLU = utilities (defensive, haven). When XLU outperforms XLI, smart money seeks shelter in safe stocks — caution signal. Current reading: Z=+1.35 — cyclical/defensive balance. No clear tilt. Action: no signal.
SPHB / SPLV
Stretch +1.60
Greed gauge
1.9972 +0.64%
◆ RULE READ · AI joins tonight
SPHB = high beta stocks, SPLV = low volatility stocks. This ratio should theoretically signal risk appetite (high beta dominates in bull market) — except that the 10-year Kerberos backtest showed an inverse direction (Z=+1.60). ⚠ Signal REJECTED by doctrine, do not trade on this ratio. Displayed here for observation only. The raw reading is: high beta vs low vol leaning.
XLF / SPY
Stretch -0.60
Banks participation
0.0719 +0.82%
◆ RULE READ · AI joins tonight
XLF = financial sector (banks + insurance), SPY = S&P 500. When banks underperform strongly, it's often that credit creation is slowing — bearish divergence for the rally. Current reading: Z=-0.60 — bank participation OK. No sector divergence. Action: no signal.

HG / GC
Stretch +2.39
Copper vs gold (growth vs fear)
0.00153 -0.30%

Copper (HG = pro-growth industrial) divided by gold (GC = recession haven). 'Doctor Copper' often gives the commodities market's verdict on growth before it shows elsewhere. Current reading: Z=+2.39 — copper leads gold. Pro-cyclical growth optimism. Action: no recession signal from commodities.
DXY
Stretch +1.13
Dollar wrecking ball
99.75 -0.11%
◆ RULE READ · AI joins tonight
DXY = dollar index (USD vs basket of major currencies). A strong dollar acts as a 'wrecking ball' on US multinationals (more expensive export) and emerging markets (more expensive USD debt). Current reading: 99.8 — DXY in range. No major dollar pressure. Action: no signal.

STLFSI4
Stretch -1.40
Systemic financial stress
-0.868 +26.21%
◆ RULE READ · AI joins tonight
St. Louis Fed financial stress index. Above 0, macro stress exceeds the historical average — alert. Above 1, it's the crisis zone. Current reading: -0.87 — stress below the average. Easy financial conditions, no macro stress signal. Action: no mandatory defensive.

Stretch -0.76
Financial stress momentum
-0.22 +691.04%
◆ RULE READ · AI joins tonight

PAIR-TRADING · MEAN-REVERT
Kerberos pair trading — Z-score plays
AUDUSD / NZDUSD
Stretch +0.38
Kerberos favorite forex pair
1.20794 -0.03%
◆ RULE READ · AI joins tonight
Highly correlated FX pair (AUD/USD vs NZD/USD = two Pacific dollars). When the divergence exceeds 2 sigma, it's a classic mean-reversion setup: the gap will close. Current reading: Z=+0.38 — pair in range. No mean-reversion opportunity right now. Action: wait.
V / MA
Stretch +1.48
Payments king pair
0.6580 +0.33%
◆ RULE READ · AI joins tonight
Visa (V) divided by Mastercard (MA). Two near-identical companies (global payments), so the divergence rarely exceeds 2 sigma — when it does, there's often a catalyst (earnings, regulatory news) creating an opportunity. Current reading: Z=+1.48 — V/MA in sync. The payments duo moves together. Action: no pair-trade opportunity.
NVDA / AMD
Stretch -1.73
Semis arbitrage
0.4011 -4.37%
◆ RULE READ · AI joins tonight
Nvidia (NVDA) divided by AMD. Two major semiconductor / AI players, normally highly correlated. When the divergence exceeds 2 sigma, one is overperforming excessively — mean-reversion opportunity. Current reading: Z=-1.73 — NVDA/AMD aligned. Both AI plays move together. Action: no opportunity.
ETH / BTC
Stretch -2.00
Crypto risk appetite
0.02619 -0.42%
◆ RULE READ · AI joins tonight
Ethereum (ETH) divided by Bitcoin (BTC). When ETH underperforms BTC strongly, it's crypto risk-off (altcoins weaken first). Frequent precursor of stress on traditional risky assets. Current reading: Z=-2.00 — ETH breaking down vs BTC. Crypto risk-off, early alert for traditional risky assets (SPX can follow within 1-2 weeks). Action: watch, hedge SPY if confluence with other signals.

EV% = win_rate × (target−entry) − (1−win_rate) × (entry−stop). Tier = T1 highest conviction. 52w% = % of 52-week high. Entry/stop/target levels live on the Trade Desk →
How to read
Heat ranking: composite of RS (relative strength vs SPY), Momentum (price action 20-60d), Breadth (% stocks above SMA), Options flow, and Attention. Use top-5 as rotation focus.
Emerging industries: high velocity = screener scores accelerating this week vs prior baseline — potential early rotation signal.
Competitor themes: token frequency × recency across top finance Substacks — useful narrative context, not a trading signal.
Best R/R: only entries with an active screener leg (PULLBACK / BOTTOM / SMART_MONEY_SURGE etc.). EV% = probabilistic edge per trade. Cross-check with weekly chart before sizing.

Reading the table — Z-Score: number of standard deviations vs rolling 100d mean. |Z| > 2 = Kerberos entry threshold, |Z| > 2.5 = extreme (best R/R on mean-reversion). %B: position within Bollinger Bands (1.0 = upper band, 0 = lower band).

Aggregate reading — the mean of Z-scores gives a global regime indicator. Positive → cross-asset overbought market (Risk-On, watch reversal). Negative → oversold (Risk-Off, possible bottoming).
How to read
Persist = number of consecutive days above |Z| > 2.0 (Kerberos threshold). Persist ≥ 3d = validated signal (the divergence has become a regime, not a spike). Persist 1-2d = to watch. Persist 0d = dead signal. Sort on this column to find established regimes worth trading.
Glossary — 18 terms to understand the dashboard
Z-Score
Number of standard deviations vs the historical mean (rolling 100 days). Z=0 = normal level · |Z|>2 = stress · |Z|>2.5 = extreme.
Bollinger Bands
Bands drawn at ±2.2σ around a 100-day moving average. When the price exits the bands = statistically extreme signal.
Backwardation
Inverted curve: immediate fear exceeds far fear (e.g. VIX > VIX3M). Short-term panic signal.
Contango
Normal situation where far fear exceeds immediate fear. Extreme contango = complacency.
Half-life
Number of days required for a gap to the mean to halve. Measures the speed of mean-reversion.
Cointegration
Two assets that move together in the long term despite short-term fluctuations. Statistical basis of pair-trading.
Mean-reversion
Strategy betting that a gap to the mean will close. Inverse of momentum (which bets on continuation).
Breadth
Width of participation in the rally. Good breadth = many stocks rising. Bad breadth = only a few mega-caps pull the index.
Hedge ratio
Quantity of asset B to short to neutralize 1 long unit of asset A. Calculated via OLS regression on historical returns.
Lead-time
Delay between a signal's appearance and the event it predicts. Structural = 30-90d · stress = 5-30d · triggers = 1-10d.
Bonferroni
Statistical correction for multiple testing. When testing 100 signals, some seem valid by chance. Bonferroni divides the significance threshold by the number of tests to eliminate these false positives.
MCPT (Monte Carlo Permutation Test)
Robustness test: data is randomly shuffled 5000-10000 times to see if the signal appears by chance. If p < 0.05, the signal is probably real.
Walk-forward
Test where the model is trained on the first years (e.g. 2010-2020) and tested on the following years (2020-2025). Avoids in-sample overfitting.
Tail-hedge
Hedge against an extreme crash (-20% or more). Bought via deeply out-of-the-money puts. SKEW measures institutional demand for this type of insurance.
Mega-caps
The 7-10 largest market caps in the SPX (AAPL, MSFT, NVDA, GOOGL, AMZN, META, TSLA). Weigh ~35% of the index.
Cap-weighted vs Equal-weighted
SPY = weighted by capitalization (mega-caps dominate). RSP = each stock weighs 0.2%. Comparing both reveals concentration.
Risk parity
Portfolio strategy that balances risk (not capital) across asset classes. Stressed when stocks/bonds correlations turn positive (inflation regime).
framework rule
Fundamental principle: a solo signal = noise, 3+ aligned signals = real edge. All panel signals are Bonferroni-validated over 15 years of SPY.
